# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 122

This is turn 122. Turns 1 through 121 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 122.

## Turn 122 — determinant machinery collapses to a rank-one Frattini bottleneck

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. Proof-level exponents mean the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is its verifier residue. 

Turn 121 realized the residual obstruction as a central target quotient. Before inserting its higher-rank transform into Proposition 4, there is a stronger simplification: **every higher-rank Frattini kernel already descends through its scalar pushouts.** 

Retain the active-block notation
\[
R=\Phi(K),\qquad B=Y/R,\qquad L=L_Y,
\]
where \(R\) is elementary abelian and lies in the boundary kernel. Put
\[
D_R=(R^\vee)^B.
\]

For every nonzero \(\lambda\in D_R\), define
\[
Y_\lambda:=Y/\ker\lambda,\qquad
p_\lambda:Y_\lambda\twoheadrightarrow B.
\]
Because \(\lambda\) is \(B\)-invariant, \(\ker\lambda\triangleleft Y\), and \(p_\lambda\) is a boundary-safe central \(C_2\)-cover.

Let
\[
m_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B)
=
\#\{f\in X_\Gamma^\beta(B):f^*[Y_\lambda]=0\}.
\]
The central-cover transform gives
\[
8m_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B)
=
\sum_{\substack{J\le Y_\lambda\\p_\lambda(J)=B}}
e_\Gamma^\beta(J).
\tag{122.1}
\]
This is exactly the rank-one transform already established in Proposition 6. 

Now
\[
[R:\ker\lambda]=2,
\]
so the marked kernel of \(Y_\lambda\) has order
\[
|L/\ker\lambda|
=
\frac{2|L|}{|R|}.
\tag{122.2}
\]
Consequently every subgroup \(J\) occurring in (122.1) satisfies
\[
|J\cap(L/\ker\lambda)|
\le
\frac{2|L|}{|R|}.
\tag{122.3}
\]

### Higher-rank Frattini kernels descend immediately

If
\[
|R|\ge4,
\]
then
\[
\frac{2|L|}{|R|}<|L|.
\]
Hence every term on the right of (122.1) is already matched by the global strong induction. Therefore
\[
m_{\Gamma_A,\lambda}(B)
=
m_{\Gamma_F,\lambda}(B)
\qquad(0\ne\lambda\in D_R).
\tag{122.4}
\]

The \(\lambda=0\) coefficient is simply the exact \(B\)-count, and \(B=Y/R\) also has strictly smaller marked kernel.

Thus outer Fourier inversion,
\[
\#\{f\in X_\Gamma^\beta(B):o_R(f)=0\}
=
\frac1{|D_R|}
\sum_{\lambda\in D_R}
\left(2m_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B)-|X_\Gamma^\beta(B)|\right),
\tag{122.5}
\]
matches automatically whenever \(|R|\ge4\). 

No radical-edge quotient, simple-head determinant, inner Fourier transform, Wall–Arf calculation, or local Kummer calculation is needed in this case.

### The only internal case is \(R\cong C_2\)

Suppose now
\[
|R|=2.
\]
Then \(R\) is automatically central, \(D_R=R^\vee\) is one-dimensional, and there is a unique nonzero character \(\lambda\).

There are exactly two possibilities.

If the radical edge \(E(\lambda)\) is nonzero, Turn 118’s pointwise argument gives
\[
m_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B)
=
\frac12|X_\Gamma^\beta(B)|.
\tag{122.6}
\]
So this branch is already closed by common-word duality.

If
\[
E(\lambda)=0,
\tag{122.7}
\]
then this is the unique non-descending situation. Since the obstruction is already scalar, outer Fourier inversion collapses:
\[
\frac12\left(
|X_\Gamma^\beta(B)|
+
2m_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B)
-
|X_\Gamma^\beta(B)|
\right)
=
m_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B).
\tag{122.8}
\]
Only here must Proposition 4 use the inner \(D_0\)-Fourier accessibility formula, determinant shearing, the candidate/local Gauss-sum comparison, and the smaller \(\Delta_{\lambda,a}\)-covers.

Hence the permanent structural trichotomy is
\[
\begin{array}{c|c}
|R|\ge4
&
\text{all scalar pushouts descend strictly by induction},
\\[2mm]
R\cong C_2,\ E(\lambda)\ne0
&
\text{pointwise half-fibre},
\\[2mm]
R\cong C_2,\ E(\lambda)=0
&
\text{terminal determinant/double-Fourier branch}.
\end{array}
\tag{122.9}
\]

This is sharper than Turn 121’s descent-or-central dichotomy. The expensive determinant machinery is not required for an arbitrary central zero-edge \(R\); it is required only for a **single central Frattini bit**.

It also means the higher-rank transform (121.9) need not be added as a separate manuscript lemma. Repeated use of the already-audited central double-cover transform closes every \(|R|\ge4\) stage and leaves precisely the rank-one bottleneck.

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved the scalar-pushout rank-reduction theorem. Every active block with \(|\Phi(K)|\ge4\) closes entirely by strict marked-kernel induction through its central \(C_2\)-pushouts. The only stage requiring determinant, Kummer, shearing, or inner-Fourier machinery is \(\Phi(K)\cong C_2\) with zero radical edge. This strictly simplifies Turn 121 and makes its new higher-rank central-transform lemma unnecessary.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun, and the manuscript still lacks independent external review. The terminal \(R=C_2,\ E=0\) branch continues to rely on the audited double-Fourier and determinant propositions.

**NEXT**: Replace Proposition 4 by the trichotomy (122.9): dispatch \(|R|\ge4\) using (122.1)–(122.5), dispatch the rank-one nonzero-edge case by the pointwise half-fibre, and retain the full determinant argument only for \(R=C_2,\ E=0\). Then freeze the proof and submit the unchanged candidate.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
